It showed much better thermals during gameplay and better performance in those reall life tasks. Though, for gaming, we'd call it a match with the iPhone because even though Xiaomi has plenty of raw power, games are just better optimized on iOS. Now, let's see what the developers have coded into these phones. [music] The Shyomi ships with Hyper OS 3 based on Android 16 right out of the box. And the iPhone is currently running iOS 26.1. Xiaomi hasn't actually said how many years of updates this Chinese release will get. And while Apple usually doesn't officially state it either, iPhones typically get OS and security updates for around 7 years. Now, let's quickly check out these UIs. HyperOS3 is fresh out of the oven, but it already looks familiar. That's because it's becoming more and more of a carbon copy of iOS with some splashes of liquid glass here and there. However, it does come with deeper personalization, letting you use AI to bring your wallpapers to life, for example. You also have a wide range of icon options and even full themes. Xiaomi's Hyper Island is basically a clone of the iPhone's dynamic island. Though to be fair, the iPhone definitely got its inspiration from Honor's Smart Capsule in the first place. Of course, we've also got some AI features like in the gallery to help you create that perfect shot. Since this phone comes with a Chinese ROM, one thing that could be a drawback for some users is the missing Google Play services. You can still install the Play Store and other Google apps, but features like Android Auto and Quick Share just won't work. iOS 26 is rocking the new liquid glass interface with Windows arrow vibes. Here we get fluid animations, updated icons, and deeper personalization that even extends to the camera UI. We've also got some AI features like call screening for people with anxiety for answering the phone. Siri with chat GPT integration, which now actually replies with actual information and visual intelligence for camera or screenshotbased searches. Finally, the improved Playground app lets you create even more ridiculous photos. However, we still find it kind of annoying that there's no global back gesture, so you still have to guess in which app it works and in which it doesn't. For creators, you get Pro Raw for deep photo editing and ProRes RAW for even greater control over your video footage in post-prouction. But now, let's move on to our last category. Energizing these devices, Xiaomi has an absolute unit of a battery rated at 7,500 mAh, while our iPhone only has 4,823 mAh since it is the international version. For wired charging, Xiaomi is crushing it with 100 W, while Apple doesn't even officially tell you the wattage number for the iPhone. [music] For wireless charging, Xiaomi is twice as fast as the iPhone at an impressive 50 W. Both phones do support reverse wired and wireless charging. However, the iPhone can only use that reverse wireless charge feature to top up a MagSafe battery pack while it is attached to the phone and only when the phone itself is [music] plugged in and actively charging via a cable. In our charging test using a 100 W charger, Xiaomi went from zero to hero in just 47 minutes, peaking at 76 watts of power. The iPhone, while definitely faster than previous models, took a full 1 hour and 26 minutes to finish, only reaching 37 watts. As an extra, Xiaomi now supports the power delivery PPS charging standard, which means you don't necessarily have to buy [music] a specific Xiaomi branded charger to get those super fast charging speeds. Now, the single biggest issue with Xiaomi isn't the phone itself. It's actually is worldwide availability or more like total unavailability since right now it's exclusively a Chinese market release over there. It retails for around $850. However, if you want to import one abroad, those import fees quickly beef that up to about $1,000. On the other hand, the iPhone starts at $1,199. That's a roughly $199 gap between these two phones. And that's money you could totally use to buy a 22in Laboo. So to wrap things up, it's more than obvious that Xiaomi strategy is to directly compete with the iPhone 17 Pro Max. And to be totally fair, it doesn't just do it in name and design. The Xiaomi actually brings some heavyduty hardware like a great main display, a practical and genuinely useful secondary one, good speakers, a massive battery, and most importantly, tons of performance that actually showed to be better than the iPhones in most of our tests. However, the cameras are only great on paper because the results are just all right. The software is also plain unoriginal, and most importantly, its high import price and current market unavailability essentially cross it off the list for many buyers. title: iPhone 17 Pro: La REALIDAD después de 1 mes de uso...
transcript: Hello everyone, welcome to a new video on the channel.  Today I bring you the complete review of the iPhone 17 Pro, a device that promises to make a significant leap in virtually every aspect.  Let's see what changes, what improvements it brings that aren't so great, and in the end, whether it's really worth making the leap or if it's better to wait.  The unboxing experience is very similar to the previous version.  The box is white.  The colors have changed with the product image .  When we open it we will find the phone, the braided USB Type-C cable which feels of good quality, it is very similar to the previous version and these papers that always come in Apple boxes. With a notable redesign, the iPhone 17 Pro has seen Apple abandon the titanium frames seen in previous generations and switch to a unibody aluminum construction combined with glass, which improves heat dissipation thanks to an internal vapor chamber. The screen is a 6.3-inch OLED Super Retina XDR with a 120Hz refresh rate, a resolution of 2,622 by 1206 pixels, and great visual quality.  In terms of weight and dimensions, this body is somewhat larger than the previous one, but it is still perfectly manageable with one hand.  The dimensions are 150 by 71.9 by 8.8 mm and the weight is 206 g. Honestly, I prefer the weight and dimensions of the iPhone Air.  I find this phone much more comfortable, but if we have to compare the Pro versions, of course the 17 Pro is going to be the best option.  We have water resistance and P68 which will allow you to submerge it 6m for 30 minutes, which I do not recommend at all because it voids the warranty.  In terms of design, the most visible change is the rear camera, an island that runs the entire width of the phone with three rear sensors of 48 megapixels each .  At the bottom we have a glass plate that is integrated into the entire body and also the Apple logo.  It's a decision I really liked because we no longer have so much glass on the back.  This means that if you drop it from that side, it won't break so easily.  The aluminum frame facilitates greater heat dissipation and together with the vapor chamber reduces overheating under heavy load.  I like this decision, since we're prioritizing functionality over design, but I still think this iPhone isn't as pretty as previous versions.  Another thing to keep in mind is that many users have reported that their iPhones have been scratched or are changing color, specifically the orange version.  I honestly haven't used it without a case.  Since I bought it, I put a case on it and spent a few days testing to see if it would get scratched without the case, but I honestly didn't find any scratches.  If you want to get rid of this problem, definitely put a case and screen protector on it, just like you've always done on all your iPhones.  And if you don't want to use a case, the best color for that will be silver, because the scratches will be less noticeable .  We have exactly the same arrangement of buttons on the edges.  On the right side is the power button, also the camera control, on the left side the action button and the volume up and down buttons.  On the bottom edge we have the type C connector, the speaker, the microphone, and on the top edge we have nothing, and on all edges we will have the lines for the antennas.  What I have noticed quickly in this generation is that if you have the phone without a case, it will feel a bit more ergonomic in your hand thanks to these edges because they are a bit more rounded.  In terms of audio quality, this is truly one of the best phones for playing multimedia.  They sound great through the speakers .  We have stereo sound.  I still need to work out the volume, but overall it sounds pretty good.  The bass and low frequencies are also very pronounced.  All the sound seems clear to me.  Right now I'm going to give you a sample so you can get an idea of ​​what it sounds like. [Music] The 17 Pro's screen lives up to expectations with its 120Hz refresh rate. This means smoother scrolling, better touch response, and Oleda technology ensures deep blacks and vibrant colors. Furthermore, the maximum brightness has been increased compared to the previous generation, which greatly improves the visual experience outdoors in bright light. Specifically, we have 3,000 nits on the screen, and this will make everything look perfect when there is too much light, for example, on a midday day. One thing I liked is that the edges are kept quite trimmed, and at the top we have the Dynamic Island which will help you unlock the phone quickly.  I would have liked to see a reduction or even elimination of Dynamic Island, but that's something we'll probably see in future versions.  The overall user experience, navigation, scrolling, and interactions feel ultra-smooth, from watching videos to playing games.  Everything is accompanied by a great fluidity that is expected nowadays, but not always achieved.  The viewing angles are very good and the screen experience really makes it feel very similar to the previous version.  If you're coming from the iPhone 16, I don't think you'll notice much of a difference. Internally we have Apple's Jeep A19 Pro built on 3 nanm accompanied by a higher amount of RAM.  The Pro model has 12 GB compared to the 8 GB of the standard model, and storage starts at 256 GB, which is one of the best I've liked this year because we no longer have the 128 GB version and Apple kept the price the same. The Apple six-core GPU graphics card features WiFi 7, Bluetooth 6.0, GPS, NFC, and USB Type-C 3.2 Gen 2 with DisplayPort capability.  Thanks to the vapor chamber and new thermal design, this iPhone handles prolonged workloads better without sacrificing performance.  In practice, this means that demanding games, video editing, or heavy multitasking can be handled without the computer overheating or losing speed.  As for the software, it comes with iOS 26, which provides better camera tools and a support cycle that is expected to be longer thanks to the more powerful hardware.   You could estimate about 7 years of updates.  Apple usually does, but this is the approximate time they support their devices.  The experience of using it is very similar to previous versions.  The difference lies specifically in the design with all this talk about transparencies, and another important thing is that Apple Intelligence has received virtually no updates. We have the same experience as previous versions, and I haven't liked this much because I would expect better integration of Apple Intelligence with all the system tools, which is practically nonexistent and leaves Apple Intelligence far behind the competition, specifically on Android. We have two versions of the battery; most come in SIM, which is the version that will be sold in the United States, and it has 42 mAh.  In my tests I can tell you the following: the battery has surprised me in a good way because it is very good considering how small this phone is. The iPhone 17 Pro really wants to shine in camera and video, with its three 48-megapixel rear lenses.  There is something important to mention here, and that is that we share two cameras with the previous version, the main one and also the ultra-wide one.  The difference lies in the telephoto lens, which is now 48 megapixels. This gives the iPhone 17 Pro great versatility thanks to the 4X optical zoom directly on the telephoto lens, providing a very interesting capture range, as well as better detail thanks to a larger sensor.  The front camera has 18 megapixels with a center stage that allows for improvements in selfies.  And my favorite part about this front camera is that now in all versions of the iPhone 17 series and also the L we will be able to record in vertical and horizontal format without moving the phone.  It is also capable of recording video in professional formats such as Progress RAW or dual recording with the front and rear cameras in some scenarios.  Without a doubt, one of the strongest points and the biggest generational leap compared to the 16 is the complete redesign of the cameras.  Although they may seem similar at first glance, internally there are important changes that improve the quality, versatility and overall performance of photography and video.  Starting with the main sensor, we still have 48 megapixels and larger 1.22-micron pixels, which allows us to capture more light, reduce noise, and improve dynamic range, especially in low-light situations. Textures on leather, clothing, and metallic surfaces look more natural and with less artificial processing.  One important thing to mention is that the best results in both good and low light conditions can be obtained with the main camera. We even have other modes like night mode, which will allow you to take long exposure photos and thus get better results.  The ultra-wide lens also received significant improvements, maintaining the 48 megapixels, but with better edge distortion correction .  Finally, Apple has updated the telephoto lens, and now we have a 4X optical zoom lens with a tetraprism lens that improves sharpness, even at long distances.  The difference compared to the zoom of the 16 Pro is very noticeable; portraits from afar, landscape shots, or 4X videos look sharper and without that watercolor effect that we could see in previous versions.  It's not exactly the best telephoto lens on the market.  Among rivals we might find better options in real life, from OPPO, from Samsung, but even so I could tell you that it is the best telephoto lens that Apple has so far.  Another thing I'd like to highlight is that Apple's website says we have many optically high-quality lenses, and yes, we do , but not as many as Apple claims, because there are only three.  And this is a sort of half-truth because it is true that we have several optical qualities, specifically the ultra-wide, wide-angle and telephoto, but in between them we can make crops, specifically a 2X with the main camera and an 8X with the telephoto lens.  But these different distances are not optical because they are crops of the 48- megapixel sensors and of course, like any crop, we are going to lose detail.  In the video we will find many important differences this year.  To begin, we will be able to record at 4K up to 120 frames per second, just like the previous version.  And here we don't find so many differences.  The important thing lies in the recording methods, because now we have the option to record in HDR Pros, prestr RAW, Genlock and Apple Logarithmic 2, which are very professional recording methods and unless you are interested in or need to record in those types of formats, you will find a use for it.  But if you're not that type of user, don't worry because, in short, the video quality is very good, both on the main and front cameras.  This is a video test with the front camera in 4K 30 frames per second with the iPhone 17 Pro and I find it much more comfortable to record this way because I have the phone in portrait mode, but I'm recording in landscape mode and it's definitely a huge improvement that the front camera of this phone has undergone.  I also like what I'm seeing on screen in terms of quality, colors, sharpness, and dynamic range. I think it's great, but of course I'd like to hear your thoughts in the comments about the front camera on this iPhone 17 Pro. As for the price and availability, I believe you can already get it anywhere in the world.  It's becoming relatively easy to get iPhones, and the price will start at $10.99 before tax.   One of the things I liked most about the iPhone 17 Pro is its performance. Honestly, I have no complaints here , everything works perfectly, very smoothly, I haven't had any problems.  I think the heat dissipation is better on this phone, even though we have less durable construction materials than the previous version, because titanium is definitely much more durable and more beautiful in my opinion.  But aluminum makes a very good work surface.  The camera is more versatile.  Now it offers a 4X zoom, which is one of the things I liked the most.  The front camera has improved in every way, and this makes the experience with both the main and front cameras a good duo, and I think that for creating content, simply documenting your life, they will be excellent tools.  The screen is fluid, 120 Hz, has good colors, and great visual quality.  To be honest, I have n't noticed much of a difference from the previous version.  What I did n't like about the iPhone 17 Pro is the limited color options, as well as the build materials. Being made of aluminum makes it better at heat dissipation, but at the same time it's easier to scratch and also to get the phone looking damaged, and this probably won't age well over time.  Perhaps the best option, if you're very concerned about this issue, is to buy the silver version, because that's the one that wo n't show as much wear and tear over time.  Another thing I did n't like as much is that the Face ID is still as large as it was in previous versions.  I was hoping it would get smaller, but that was n't the case in this version.  And of course, as could not be missing, there is Apple Intelligence, which is not at the level of all the artificial intelligence that exists in the Android environment.  I think this is a very significant limitation that makes me miss my S25 Ultra even when I've been using the iPhone 17 Pro. I still think it's a great phone, and I've really enjoyed using it for the past few days.  Of course I'll continue to bring more videos to the channel with updates on this device, but I think this phone is one of the best compact phones on the market. I hope you enjoyed this video and found it helpful in making a better purchase decision.  If so, don't forget to like and subscribe to the channel. Let me know in the comments what interests you most about the iPhone 17 Pro. I'll see you in the next video.

title: iPhone 17 Pro Max Vs 16 Pro Max Camera Test: Selfie, Portrait, Zoom, Low Light, & Video Quality
transcript: While testing the iPhone cameras, I found  a major problem on the 17 Pro Max that   no one is talking about. While capturing  low-light videos, I noticed those jitters,   and once you see it, you can’t unsee it.  And trust me, this is not what you expect   from a flagship & this wasn’t the only  issue I noticed. But more on that later. In this video, I'm comparing the cameras of the  iPhone 17 Pro Max and the iPhone 16 Pro Max.   I’m actually using the iPhone 16 Pro here,  but since both the Pro and Pro Max have   the same cameras and processor, you’ll  get identical results either way. This   video is being recorded from their front camera at  4k@60fps, and this is the sound and video quality. The 17 Pro Max features a new 18  megapixel center stage sensor this year,   which allows me to record landscape  videos without rotating the phone. And   this works with all selfie modes,  whether its normal or portrait. Both phones can record cinematic  videos from the front at 4k@30fps.   Try to notice how well they can  manage the blur and edge detection. These are some selfie samples I captured. Personally, I haven't had any issues with heavy [music] use of this phone and it has really lasted me a long time throughout the day.  By the evening I had about 20-30% battery charge left .  The 17th Max also features a new cooling chamber that disperses [music] heat throughout the phone and keeps it from getting too hot.  Now, of course, we'll talk about the camera.  Like the previous model, the camera consists of three rear modules.  Moreover, as before, they do not work separately, but in a complex hardware and software system.  That is, for example, if you close one eye, you will still be able to take a picture , [music] but it will be worse.  As a result, it becomes increasingly difficult to understand what each module is capable of individually , and it is essentially pointless.  The logic here is this: the user simply shoots, choosing the angle, and the system itself decides which modules to use for the task at hand .  I also liked the dual camera shooting feature.  I think this would be great for giving someone a gift and recording their reaction right away. Also, I think this would be suitable for a concert, so that you could film both yourself and the performance.  So you can play to your heart's content .  [music] Let's move on. Many shooting formats have also emerged.  They are on top.  If you are not very familiar with these settings, you can turn to the [music] internet.   There are already a lot of camera tests there, and people will have something to advise you.  Also, to sum it up, I can say from my own experience that this phone is suitable for content creators, video bloggers, or if you just want the brightest, smoothest, and most durable smartphone.  And with you was Kirill, the Techri team.  If you liked the video, give it a like and leave a comment. See you soon.  y

title: WARNING: Apple’s clear case RUINED my iPhone 17 Pro Max!
transcript: Apple's new iPhone 17 Pro models have a big problem. This year, the Pro versions use aluminum. It's lighter and it can run cooler, but it dents and scratches more easily. Apple hasn't used aluminum in their top tier phones since the iPhone 8 Plus back in 2017. If you're like me, you've trusted a thin case for years. This time, that wasn't enough. I love Apple products, but in this video, I'm going to show you several reasons why the official Apple clear case is not worth purchasing this year for your iPhone 17 Pro models. Let's start with the overall design and feel. The 2025 Apple Clear Case looks different compared to previous years. [music] The case is easy to put on, but it's surprisingly difficult to take off, and if you rush it, you could leave scuffs on the aluminum frame. Apple says it uses polycarbonate with flexible sides, but in the hand, it isn't as flexible as I'd like. It's sold as a clear case, but it doesn't look truly clear because of the large solid square on the back that hides the [music] magnets and the slightly misaligned Apple logo. There is a raised lip around the front screen and around the camera plateau on the back. These help with face down protection, but they are not high enough to give [music] me confidence in a real drop. One thing I do like is the open section along the bottom side of the front lip. It keeps the swipe up gesture clean and gets closer to that almost naked iPhone feel. The buttons are comfortable, responsive, and have a satisfying click. The bottom of the case is open, so the speakers and charging port are not blocked. That means clean sound and easy cable insertion, which can be an issue on thicker cases. In terms [music] of grip, the case is a bit slippery. You'll want to be more careful when pulling the phone from a pocket or car seat. A simple textured edge would help a lot in a future version. Comfort is solid because the case is thin. That's a big reason I've always liked slim cases. I want protection, but I still want the phone to feel close to how it feels without a case. Mag Safe performance is about the same as a naked iPhone, and that's what I would expect at this point. The built-in magnets keep charging alignment consistent and hold fine for most accessories. If a case doesn't have MagSafe built-in, you usually lose strength. So, I'm glad Apple includes it here. Now, let's talk about drop protection, which is where this clear case really disappointed me. About a week after getting my 17 Pro Max, I got out of my car and the phone slipped from my pocket. It landed face first on asphalt from roughly three feet high. The screen was fine, likely thanks to the front lip, but when I took the case off, I found dents or scuff marks around all four corners of the iPhone, even though those corners were covered by the case. That surprised me because I've used Apple clear cases on many of my previous iPhones without any problems. One simple three-foot drop caused visible damage to the phone's aluminum edges. I didn't realize how much more prone aluminum was to dents compared to stainless steel or titanium, even as the alloys have improved over time. This Apple clear case just doesn't add enough shock absorption at the corners to protect an aluminum pro in everyday drops. Now, before we get to the final part of this video, if this review has been valuable to you, hit subscribe. We're pushing for 1,000 subscribers, and when we get there, I'm sending an Apple related gift to one of you. Subscribing is free, and it helps me keep testing gear so you don't waste money. Now, let's get back to the video. Beyond the weak drop performance, I also wish this case had a built-in kickstand. I don't want to buy a separate MagSafe stand just to watch videos, and adding a stand accessory can block other MagSafe attachments I use. The case tries to stay minimal, but it ends up sacrificing usefulness. So, would I recommend this case? If you have an aluminum iPhone 17 Pro or Pro Max, absolutely not. Apple needs [music] to make several changes for this to be a smart buy. Taller lips around the screen and camera, a more flexible material mix to increase bounce on impact, and reinforced corners to absorb those drops. A built-in kickstand would also add real everyday value. Apple's clear case looks sleek on day one, but it fails at the most basic job, which is drop protection. So, I'll be looking at other options. If you want to see my review of a nearly perfect, rugged iPhone 17 Pro Max case, tap on the video on the screen now. Thanks for watching, and I'll see you guys in the next one.

title: SAMSUNG S26 ULTRA !🔥 ADIEU IPHONE 17 PRO MAX #shorts #samsungs26ultra
transcript: This is the S25 Ultra and this is the future S26 Ultra.  Samsung is preparing its next premium model but beware, [music] there are three big points that may not be to everyone's liking. Firstly, the price will go up.  Leaks suggest a price increase of $50 to $100, or even more on certain configurations.  So if the S25 Ultra started at around €1469, we can clearly expect the S26 Ultra to be above €1500.  And that's when things really start to sting.  Secondly, the battery will remain at 5000 mAh.  But most importantly, Samsung still does not use carbon silicon technology, which would allow increased capacity without making the phone thicker.  And Chinese brands are already doing that.  Today we see smartphones with 6000, 7000 or even 7500 mAh processors, like the new Oppo Fan X 9 Pro for example.  So yes, we could have had much better and that's frankly a shame. title: Probamos la funda de silicona de Apple para iPhone 17 Pro
transcript: Hello, welcome to the Apple Talking Channel with the new iPhone 17 Pro. Apple has released several interesting accessories like the crossbody strap and the tech-braided case, but today I want to focus on one of the most classic and popular: the silicone case with Maxave, which is back with new colors and some interesting changes. Apple offers this case in five colors: yellow, blue, fog, moss, and black, which is the shade you're seeing on this screen.  It is made entirely of silicone, 45% of which comes from recycled material.  The buttons are also made of silicone and have a very pleasant feel, with good response when pressed.  The dedicated camera button works perfectly, and on the back we find the camera mount where the edges protrude slightly. This eliminates the typical wobble problem when we leave the iPhone on a surface, on a table, and also protects the rear lenses.  At first glance, this edge gives the impression of being metallic.  The front edge protrudes just enough so that if we leave the phone face down, the screen does not touch the surface.  At the bottom there are perforations for the speaker, microphones and USBC charging port.   It also has a slot to attach the new crossbody strap, sold separately, which allows you to hang your iPhone. One positive point is that this case doesn't slip on surfaces, which is appreciated.  Inside it has a soft touch that conveys quality and is fully compatible with Maxavef and wireless charging.  It offers no resistance when putting it on or taking it off and is quite easy to adjust.  The case costs €59 and if we add the crossbody strap we have to add another €69, which leaves a set of €128.  Personally, I think it's an excessive price for a silicone case.  On Amazon it has an average rating of 4.4 stars and the price is usually around €56 with a €3 discount.  I've noticed that with this case the iPhone 17 Pro gets hotter, especially during charging, although with daily use I don't really notice that much.  What I liked most was the compatibility with Maxif and the feel, which is very soft.  What I liked least is clearly the price, which is still high for such a simple accessory that we can find for much less money in other stores. In summary, the Maxave silicone case for the Apple iPhone 17 Pro stands out for its good construction, a very pleasant feel, and the protection it offers to both the camera and the screen.  However, its price remains difficult to justify, especially when you add the crossbody strap.  It's a practical, elegant, and secure case, but clearly expensive for what it offers.

title: iPhone 17 Pro Max AirPlay Not Working? - How to Fix!
transcript: Is AirPlay not working on your iPhone 17, 17 Pro, or Pro Max? You can't cast media to other devices like your Mac or smart TVs? They don't show up on AirPlay, keeps loading and trying to connect, or you get the unable to connect error. Here's how you get AirPlay to work in minutes. First of all, make sure both your devices are connected to the same Wi-Fi network. And you should also go to settings general then airplane and continuity. First open automatically airplane and keep it to either ask or automatic. Also scroll all the way to the bottom and turn on airplane receiver. It can be required for any connection type even though it says vision pro at the bottom. Also, you need to double check that AirPlay is also enabled on the other device that you're trying to connect. For example, if you're having trouble AirPlaying to a Mac device, then you need to go to your Mac devices airdrop and handoff settings, turn on AirPlay receiver, click on allow Air Play 4, and select anyone on the same network or everyone. If these settings weren't the issue, then it should give both your iPhone and the other device a forced restart. To force restart your iPhone, quickly press and release the volume up, then the volume down button, and right after that, hold down the side button as long as the Apple logo doesn't appear. Then try AirPlay once again. And it's also recommended that you keep your iPhone updated to the latest iOS. In case you can't AirPlay content just from a specific app, then you probably have location disabled for that settings. Then let's open up settings. Scroll all the way down to apps. Now find the app that's causing trouble. Open it and toggle on local network. Now you should be able to use AirPlay on that app. In case it still isn't working for you, then you can try AirPlay to a different device. If it's able to do that, then it's probably not your iPhone. But if your iPhone can't AirPlay to any device, then let's try opening settings. Go to general and scroll all the way down to transfer reset reset and from these options select reset network settings. This won't erase any data but you'll need to connect back to Wi-Fi. If you can, you can also go to reset all settings for peace of mind. And after following these steps, you should be able to AirPlay your iPhone 17 to any compatible device. Let us know which portion helped and I'll see you around.
